shadow_mode.overview: PARWA's Shadow Mode is a 3-tier safety system that controls how Jarvis executes actions on behalf of clients. It exists to ensure AI actions are safe, transparent, and controllable. The system uses a 4-layer decision architecture to determine the appropriate mode for every individual action.

shadow.name: Shadow Mode
shadow.description: Jarvis shows what it would do, but nothing is actually executed. The client sees the full recommendation including risk assessment, and must manually approve before any action is taken.
shadow.when_used: New clients during onboarding, low-confidence actions, actions Jarvis has never performed before, or when the client explicitly requests shadow mode for a category.
shadow.example: Jarvis wants to process a $500 refund. Risk score is 0.8 (high). System puts it in shadow mode. Client sees: 'Jarvis recommends refunding $500 to customer X for order #12345. Risk: High. Reason: Amount exceeds typical refund pattern.' Client clicks Approve or Reject.

supervised.name: Supervised Mode
supervised.description: Jarvis executes low-confidence actions in shadow (preview mode) and asks for approval on medium-to-high risk actions. Low-risk actions may auto-execute with an undo option.
supervised.when_used: Clients who have been using shadow mode for a while and Jarvis has built confidence, but the client still wants oversight on important actions.
supervised.example: Jarvis auto-sends a simple order status reply (risk 0.1, low) but shows a refund recommendation (risk 0.6, medium) for approval. Client can see both in the approvals queue.

graduated.name: Graduated Mode
graduated.description: Jarvis auto-executes low-risk actions and shows them in the undo queue. High-risk actions still go to shadow/approval. Auto-approved actions can be undone within a configurable time window.
graduated.when_used: Experienced clients where Jarvis has learned their patterns well and has high confidence scores.
graduated.example: Jarvis auto-processes a $15 refund (risk 0.05, low). It appears in the undo queue with a 30-minute countdown. Client can click 'Undo' within that window, or let it stand.

layer_1_jarvis_ai_risk_assessment.name: Jarvis AI Risk Assessment
layer_1_jarvis_ai_risk_assessment.description: Jarvis evaluates every action before execution. It considers: confidence in the response, financial impact, data sensitivity, customer impact, and whether it has seen similar actions succeed before.
layer_1_jarvis_ai_risk_assessment.output: A risk score from 0.0 (no risk) to 1.0 (maximum risk), plus a mode recommendation.
layer_1_jarvis_ai_risk_assessment.rules: If risk >= 0.7 → always shadow. If risk 0.3-0.7 → shadow or supervised based on client preference. If risk < 0.3 → auto-execute (in supervised/graduated mode).

layer_2_client_preferences.name: Client Preferences
layer_2_client_preferences.description: The client can set preferences per action category. These can be set two ways: through the Dashboard Settings UI, or by talking to Jarvis in chat. Both methods sync to the same database table (shadow_preferences).
examples: 'Jarvis, always put refunds in shadow mode' → shadow_preferences updated for 'refund' category
'Always ask me before sending SMS' → shadow_preferences updated for 'sms' category
UI toggle: set 'email_reply' to 'graduated' → shadow_preferences updated
These preferences OVERRIDE Jarvis's risk assessment if set
layer_2_client_preferences.sync: Changes made in UI appear in Jarvis context within seconds via WebSocket. Changes made via Jarvis chat update the UI in real-time.

layer_3_learned_behavior.name: Learned Behavior
layer_3_learned_behavior.description: Over time, Jarvis learns client patterns. If a client consistently rejects auto-approved refunds above $200, Jarvis will start putting those in shadow mode proactively, even if the risk score is low.
layer_3_learned_behavior.mechanism: Jarvis tracks approval/rejection patterns per action category and adjusts its behavior. This layer does NOT override Layer 2 (client preferences) or Layer 4 (hard safety floor).
examples: Client rejects 5 out of 5 auto-approved refunds > $200 → Jarvis learns to shadow those
Client always approves low-risk order status replies → Jarvis auto-executes those confidently

layer_4_hard_safety_floor.name: Hard Safety Floor
layer_4_hard_safety_floor.description: Certain actions ALWAYS require human approval, regardless of mode, client preference, or Jarvis confidence. This layer cannot be overridden by anyone — not even Jarvis, not even the client, not even in Graduated mode.
actions: Payment or refund above configurable threshold
Any action affecting multiple customers simultaneously
layer_4_hard_safety_floor.rule: These actions go directly to the approvals queue in shadow mode, regardless of the current system mode.

dual_control.concept: Clients can control shadow mode two ways: (1) Dashboard Settings UI page, (2) Talking to Jarvis in chat. Both methods are equally important — neither is secondary.
dual_control.how_it_works: Both UI and Jarvis write to the same shadow_preferences database table. When one interface makes a change, the other is notified via WebSocket and updates in real-time.
jarvis_commands: what is my current shadow mode?
why was this action put in shadow mode?

undo_system.description: In Supervised and Graduated modes, auto-approved actions have an undo window. Clients can undo recent actions within this window.
undo_system.how_it_works: When Jarvis auto-executes an action, it is logged in the undo_log table with a timestamp. The client has a configurable time window (default: 30 minutes) to undo it. The undo reverses the action if possible, or flags it for manual review.
undo_system.ui_access: Dashboard shows a list of recent auto-approved actions with 'Undo' buttons and countdown timers.
undo_system.jarvis_access: Client can say 'undo the last refund' or 'undo action #123' in chat.
undo_system.limitations: Some actions cannot be undone after execution (e.g., SMS already sent, email already delivered). These show a warning before auto-approval.

dashboard_ui_components.mode_badge: Small colored badge in dashboard header showing current global mode. Orange = Shadow, Yellow = Supervised, Green = Graduated. Clicking opens mode selector or links to settings.
dashboard_ui_components.approvals_queue: Page at /dashboard/approvals listing all pending shadow actions. Each item shows: action type, AI recommendation, risk score, timestamp. Manager can Approve/Reject/Modify/View Details. Batch approve available.
dashboard_ui_components.undo_interface: Shows recent auto-approved actions still within undo window. Each has Undo button with countdown timer.
dashboard_ui_components.shadow_log: Audit trail at /dashboard/shadow-log. Full history of all shadow mode actions. Filterable by date, type, mode, outcome. Export to CSV available.
dashboard_ui_components.settings_panel: Per-action-category mode configuration. Syncs with Jarvis. Set default mode for each action type.
dashboard_ui_components.what_if_simulator: Preview what mode Jarvis would assign to a hypothetical action. Enter action details → see predicted risk score and mode.

database_tables.shadow_log: Records every action that went through shadow mode. Fields: id, company_id, action_type, action_payload (JSONB), jarvis_risk_score, mode, manager_decision, manager_note, resolved_at, created_at.
database_tables.undo_log: Records actions that were auto-approved but then undone. Fields: id, shadow_log_id, company_id, original_action (JSONB), undo_reason, undo_window_used, created_at.
database_tables.shadow_preferences: Client-set preferences synced between UI and Jarvis. Fields: id, company_id, action_category, preferred_mode, set_via (ui/jarvis), updated_at. Unique on (company_id, action_category).

GET: /api/shadow/log — Get shadow log / audit trail (paginated, filterable)
/api/shadow/pending — Get pending approvals count and list
POST: /api/shadow/evaluate — Jarvis evaluates an action's risk score

migration_notes: shadow_preferences table needs to be created before dual control works
undo_log table references shadow_log, so shadow_log must exist first
shadow_preferences.set_via field tracks whether change came from 'ui' or 'jarvis'
Risk score threshold (0.7 for forced shadow, 0.3 for auto-execute) should be configurable per company
Undo window duration should be configurable per company (default 30 minutes)
